reforms.description: The Slovenian government is considering the introduction of an effective innovation policy supported by multi-purpose centres to enhance the business environment and foster entrepreneurship, while also aiming to optimize collaborative links between the research community, the business sector, and the State to strengthen these connections.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the business environment and fostering entrepreneurship is expected to raise productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is considering and planning these initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform aims to significantly improve the innovation ecosystem through collaboration and support mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: The quality of Slovenia’s future business environment depends, to a large extent, upon the introduction of an effective innovation policy, which could be supported by multi-purpose centres.

reforms.description: The Slovenian government implemented a fiscal stimulus package amounting to EUR800 million, which includes measures to increase expenditure in research and education, alongside the National Research and Development Programme 2006-10 that aims to rebalance public R&D spending by increasing the technology-oriented share from 10% in 2007 to 45% by 2010.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D expenditure is expected to improve the growth potential of the economy and its resilience.
reforms.status_evidence: The fiscal stimulus package and the R&D programme have been put into action.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in public R&D funding and allocation.
reforms.source_quote: The National Research and Development Programme 2006-10 (NRDP) also aims at rebalancing the structure of public R&D spending, raising the technology-oriented share in public R&D spending from 10% in 2007 to 45% by 2010.

reforms.description: Slovenia introduced tax incentives for research and development investment, allowing firms to deduct 30% of the invested amount from their tax base, up to EUR 30,000, as part of a broader incentive aimed at enhancing the country's innovation capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Tax incentives are designed to encourage private sector investment in R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax incentives have been successfully introduced and are in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of tax incentives represents a significant but narrower reform in the innovation support system.
reforms.source_quote: In 2006, incentives for research and development investment were introduced, allowing 30% of the invested amount to be deducted from the tax base, up to EUR 30,000.

reforms.description: The government established a public venture capital company in 2007 to support high-growth SMEs through public/private partnerships, alongside the introduction of tax relief for venture capital investments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting high-growth SMEs is expected to enhance innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The public venture capital company has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a public venture capital company represents a significant initiative to support the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: In 2007, the government adopted the Venture Capital Company Act, introducing tax relief for venture capital investment in high-growth SMEs.
